blocks.text: Re: Portfolio Valuation for Perkins Group

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} has engaged Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP to appraise the commercial properties comprising the Perkins Group portfolio. The engagement covers each parcel identified in the schedule furnished by your office, and that schedule is the controlling statement of scope unless amended in writing. The intended use of our reports is credit and portfolio decision-making by the client and its lenders. The intended users are identified in each report's certification.

Each property will be inspected. We will develop the income, sales comparison, and cost approaches as the character of each parcel warrants, and we will reconcile the results in a self-contained appraisal report for each property. All work will conform to the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice and to the firm's internal review procedures. Conclusions will rest on documented comparables and on conditions observed at inspection, and each report will state what was relied upon.

Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser, will direct the fieldwork and the reconciliation of each file. Marcus Burnett, Research Analyst, will assemble and verify the market data supporting each approach. David Ellison, Staff Appraiser, will conduct the inspections and maintain the working files. I will review every report and sign each certification. Requests for records and for access to the properties will be directed through your office.

Fieldwork will commence on {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}, subject to access and to receipt of the rent rolls, operating statements, and title information for each parcel. The fee and the terms of payment are set out on the execution page of this letter, where they are executed by counsel. Consistent with the firm's practice, they are not restated in the body.
If the foregoing accurately states the engagement, please sign on the execution page and return one executed copy to this office. Questions may be directed to me.
